name: RELLENO DE AGUACATE CON ACKEE & VACALAO (STUFFED AVOCADO WITH ACKEE AND SALT FISH)
intro: Ackee and salt fish is a popular Jamaican dish normally served for breakfast with bell peppers and onions, dumplings and callaloo. The nutty taste of ackee complements the texture and saltiness of the salt fish. Just like the Street-Style Vacalaitos , I remix this dish with hearts of palm, using avocado to hold both the ackee and vegan codfish for a more elevated brunch-time meal and to bring some freshness to the dish. When picking your avocados, make sure they are ready to eat and soft.

ingredients.item: can hearts of palm, drained and rinsed

method: Coarsely chop the hearts of palm into shredded fish–like pieces, about 1 to 1½ inches (2.5 to 4 cm) in size. Season with 1 teaspoon of the salt and the nori furikake, then set aside.

In a large skillet over medium heat, combine the olive oil, bell peppers, yellow onion, Scotch bonnet pepper and garlic, and sauté. This will release the aromatics and flavors and soften the peppers and onion.
Now, add the ackee, tomato, green onion, thyme, seasoned hearts of palm, remaining ½ teaspoon of salt and the black pepper. Stir and cook for about 5 minutes.
Cut both of your avocados in half and remove their pit, then add the ackee and salt fish mixture to the center of the avocado halves and serve.
